text:
All clean. Here's what the security pipeline now does, in order:

**Layer 1 — Heuristic (instant, zero latency)**
Catches the exact token in your screenshot and similar scams by pattern-matching the name/symbol:
- Telegram links (`t.me/`, `telegram`)
- "claim", "airdrop", "giveaway", "reward", "distribution", "whitelist", "promo"
- URLs (`http://`, `www.`, `.com`, `.io`, `.net`)
- Pipe characters `|` (used in scam names like `t.me/x | claim until...`)
- Non-ASCII / emoji in symbol
- Symbol longer than 12 chars
- Discord links

**Layer 2 — Moralis `possible_spam` flag**
Moralis maintains a curated spam database. Runs in batches of 25, covers ETH/Base/Arbitrum/Polygon/Optimism/BSC/Avalanche.

**Layer 3 — GoPlus contract security**
Full contract analysis: honeypot, `is_airdrop_scam`, hidden owner, self-destruct, sell tax, creator holds majority supply, no liquidity pool, unverified contract, etc. Now also checks `is_airdrop_scam` which GoPlus specifically has for this type of token.

**Behavior:**
- Any layer flagging a token → auto-deselected, red border, badge shows the reason
- Manually added tokens get instant heuristic check + async full scan
- Flags are deduplicated and merged across all three layers
